Pak Study MCQs · pages 451-500

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

How many Wajibat (obligatory acts) of prayer?

Correct Answer: 14
Show explanation
Explanation

There are 14 pillars of prayer which are as follows:

(1) Standing during obligatory prayers

(2) The opening takbeer (saying “Allahu akbar”)

(3) Reciting al-Faatihah

(4) Rukoo’ (bowing),

(5) Rising from bowing

(6) Standing up straight

(7) Sujood (prostration)

(8) Rising from prostration

(9) Sitting between the two prostrations

(10) Being at ease in each of these physical pillars

(11) The final tashahhud

(12) Sitting to recite the final tashahhud and the two salaams

(13) The two salaams. This means saying twice, “al-salaamu ‘alaykum wa rahmat-Allah"

(14) Follow the pillars in the stated order

[Deliberately prostrating before bowing invalidates the prayer; if done by mistake, one must bow and then prostrate.]

2

Who was the ruler of Afghanistan at the time of Soviet Invasion?

Correct Answer: Nur Muhammad Taraki
Show explanation
Explanation

Nur Muhammad Taraki

Born: 1917

Died: 1979)

Profession: revolutionary politician

He was a founding member of the People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan (PDPA).

 

3

نماز استسقاء ادا کی جاتی ہے

Correct Answer: برسات
Show explanation
Explanation
نماز استسقاء کا طریقہ
خشک سالی اور بارش نہ ہونے کے وقت میں کھلے میدان میں یہ نماز ادا کی جاتی ہے
تاکہ اللہ تعالیٰ سے عاجزی وانکساری سے گڑگڑاتے ہوئے بارش طلب کی جائے۔
اورنماز کے لیے نکلتے وقت پرانے کپڑے پہننے چاہئیں۔
4

اسلام کے سب سے پہلے موذن کون تھے؟

Correct Answer: حضرت بلال رضی اللہ تعالی عنہ
Show explanation
Explanation

سب سے پہلے آذادانہ آذان حضرت عمر فاروق رضی اللہ تعالی عنہ کے مشورہ پر دی گئی تھی۔

5

پانچویں کلمے کا نام کیا ہے

Correct Answer: استغفار
Show explanation
Explanation

اسلام کے چھ کلموں کے نام

طیب

پہلا کلمہ

شہادت

دوسرا کلمہ

تمجید

تیسرا کلمہ

توحید

چوتھا کلمہ

استغفار

پانچواں کلمہ

رد کفر

 

چھٹا کلمہ


6

حضرت ابراہیم نے بتوں کو ــــــــــــــــــــــ توڑا۔

Correct Answer: کلہاڑے سے
Show explanation
Explanation

ایک مرتبہ حضرتِ سیّدُنا ابراہیم علیہ السَّلام کی قوم کے افراد اپنے سالانہ عید میلے میں گئے،اُن کے چلے جانے کے بعد آپ علیہ السَّلام کلہاڑا لے کر بُت خانے گئے، جُھوٹے معبودوں کو دیکھ کر  توحیدِ الٰہی کے  جذبہ سے جلال میں آگئے اورایک  بڑے بُت کے سوا سارے بُتوں کو توڑ پھوڑ ڈالا، پھر کلہاڑا بڑے بُت کے کندھے پر رکھ دیا

7

اسلام کی سب سے پہلی مسجد کونسی ہے؟

Correct Answer: مسجد قباء
Show explanation
Explanation

مدینہ منورہ کے قریب ایک قصبہ تھا جس کا نام قبا تھا، جہاں مسجد کی تعمیر کی گئ قصبہ کی مناسبت سے مسجد کا نام مسجد قباء رکھا گیا۔

*********

RY 13-01-2023

8

تلاوت قرآن سے پھلے کيا پڑھنا چاھيے؟

Correct Answer: تعوز و تسميہ
Show explanation
Explanation

حدیث شریف میں قرآن شریف پڑھنے پر جو ثواب کا حکم مذکور ہے وہ نفسِ قرأت پر ہے خواہ سمجھ کر قرآن شریف پڑھا جائے یا بغیر سمجھے بشرطیکہ حروف کی ادائیگی صحیح ہو تلاوت کرنے پر حروف نہ کٹتے ہوں؛ البتہ اگر تلاوت قرآن کے وقت ترتیل کی رعایت کرلی جائے تدبر اور فہم معنی کا لحاظ کیا جائے

9

سب سے پہلے کشتی کس نے بنائی

Correct Answer: حضرت نوح علیہ السلام
Show explanation
Explanation

سب سے پہلے کشتی حضرت نوح علیہ السلام  نے بنائی

ایک عذاب جو ایک طوفان کی صورت میں حضرت نوح علیہ السلام کی قوم پر آیا،

جس میں حضرت نوح علیہ السلام نے ایک کشتی بنوا کر صالح انسانوں اور جانوروں کو سوار کیا۔

حضرت نوح علیہ السلام کی کشتی کو قوس کہتے ہیں

کشتی کی چھ منزلیں اور سات درجے تھے۔

اور ہر منزل کے نوحصے

اور یہ کشتی کوہ نصر پر جالگی۔

***

ND28-1-2023

10

غزوہ احد میں کفار مکہ کی تعداد کتنی تھی؟

Correct Answer: تین ہزار
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD, in the valley north of Mount Uhud.

The Qurayshi Meccans, led by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, commanded an army of 3,000 men toward Muhammad's stronghold in Medina.



11

غزوہ احد میں کفار مکہ کا سردار کون تھا؟

Correct Answer: ابو سفیان
Show explanation
Explanation
The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD, in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
  • Abu Sufyan commander of the infidels in  Uhad Ghazwa
  • ***
  • It was fought in 3 Hijri 
  • Ohad is the name of a mountain
1000 Muslims participated in the battle of Uhad

3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad Infidel means کفار
In the battle of Uhad Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) selected 50Skillful archers to stay on the mountain
12

غزوہ احد میں کتنے مسلمانوں نے شرکت کی تھی؟

Correct Answer: 1000
Show explanation
Explanation
The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD, in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
  • Abu Sufyan commander of the infidels in  Uhad Ghazwa
  • ***
  • It was fought in 3 Hijri 
  • Ohad is the name of a mountain
1000 Muslims participated in the battle of Uhad

3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad Infidel means کفار
In the battle of Uhad Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) selected 50Skillful archers to stay on the mountain
13

Who was the first caliph of after Holy Prophet

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakar
Show explanation
Explanation
Who was the First Caliph?

Hazrat Abu Bakar was the first caliph after Holy Prophet.

Hazrat Abu Bakar remained Chalipha 8 June 632 – 23 August 634
***

Who was the second Caliph?

Hazrat Umar(R.A.)
***
Who was the Third Caliph of Islam?

 Hazrat Usman(R.A.)
***


Who was the fourth Caliph?

 Hazrat Ali (R.A.)

Hazrat Ali(R.A.) Caliph period from 656 till 661 CE

***

Which sahabi proposed the name of Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) for the first Caliphate of Islam?

Hazrat Umar (R.A)

15

When is the first caliph in the Umayyad dynasty?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Muawiya (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Muawiya (R.A) triumphed over the Prophet Muhammad's son-in-law, ʿAlī, to become the fifth caliph

Ameer Muawiyah was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate, serving from 661 until his death. (30 years)

Ameer Muawiyah Born: 602 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia

Ameer Muawiyah Died: 680 AD, in Damascus


***

Ummayyad Dynasty started in the year 661  First great Muslim dynasty in Damascus

The Umayyad dynasty ended 750 last great Muslim dynasties.

Caliph  

661–680 Mu'awiya I (first) 

744–750 Marwan II (last)

***


dynasty succeeded the leadership of the first four caliphs:

  • Abū Bakr
  • ʿUmar I,
  • ʿUthmān,
  • and ʿAlī. 

***

ND29-1-2023

ND5-2-2023

ND10-3-2023

16

Which pillar of Islam washes sin like water?

Correct Answer: Hajj
Show explanation
Explanation

The word Hajj means to intend

hajj was the 5th fundamental pillar of Islam

Hajj is obligatory for Muslims Those who can afford

Hajj was made compulsory in which Hijrah 9th Hijrah

Three obligatory Acts of Hajj are there

hajj should be performed by Muslims at least once in a lifetime.

ND08-3-2023


17

How many days are prohibited for fasting throughout the Year?

Correct Answer: 5
Show explanation
Explanation
Fasting is haraam on the following days

1.Two days of Eid (`Eidul Fitr and `Eidul Adha.).

2.Three days after Eid al-Adha (the 11th, 12th and 13th of Dhu’l-Hijjah) also called al-Tashreeq.

Fasting was the 4th fundamental pillar of Islam

Fasting (Soam)  is declared as armor

Fasting was made obligatory in 10th Shaban,2 A.H

The function of fasting is To Make the Sacrifice of Body and Soul

Saum (fasting) resembles Hadith with a Shield

5 days are prohibited ممنوع for fasting throughout the Year

ND08-3-2023

Fasting (Soam)  is declared as armor

Fasting was made obligatory in 10th Shaban,2 A.H

The function of fasting is To Make the Sacrifice of Body and Soul

Saum (fasting) resembles Hadith with a Shield

5 days are prohibited ممنوع for fasting throughout the Year

21

What is the number of Zakat Among pillars

Correct Answer: Third
Show explanation
Explanation

There are five pillars of Islam and zakat was the third fundamental pillar of Islam

Zakat literally means Purification.

Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H.

The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.

Without the zakat act the prayer is useless

ND08-3-2023



22

Which country banned for sporting event for 4 years ?

Correct Answer: Russia
Show explanation
Explanation

Russia was banned from the world's top sporting events for four years on Monday, DECEMBER 9, 2019 including the next summer and winter Olympics and the 2022 soccer World Cup, for tampering with doping tests.


23

Who among the following won the Battle of Yermuk in 636 AD?

Correct Answer: Muslims
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Yarmouk was a major battle between the army of the Byzantine Empire and the Muslim forces under the command of Khalid bin Waleed.

The Battle of Yarmouk was fought during caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA)

Location: Yarmouk River
Dates: Aug 15, 636 AD – Aug 20, 636 AD
The result of the battle was a complete Muslim victory which ended Byzantine rule in Syria.



24

Which is the third-largest Islamic city in the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) member countries?

Correct Answer: Karachi
Show explanation
Explanation

OIC Stands for Organization of Islamic Cooperation
OIC was Founded on September 25, 1969

25 founding members of OIC at the time of its establishment.

OIC headquarters is located in Jadda.

It is the largest international organization outside of the United Nations.

The official languages of the OIC are Arabic, English, and French.

It changed its name on 28 June 2011 from the Organization of the Islamic Conference to its current name.

Total Current member= 57

Jakarta is the largest city in the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) member countries

26

The organization of Islamic cooperation (OIC) also known as the organization of Islamic conference formula was created in

Correct Answer: 1969
Show explanation
Explanation
OIC Stands for Organization of Islamic Cooperation
OIC was Founded on September 25, 1969

25 founding members of OIC at the time of its establishment.

OIC headquarters is located in Jadda.

It is the largest international organization outside of the United Nations.

The official languages of the OIC are Arabic, English, and French.

It changed its name on 28 June 2011 from the Organization of the Islamic Conference to its current name.

Total Current member= 57

Jakarta is the largest city in the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) member countries

What is sehri called in Arabic?

In which year of hijra was the fasting of Ramadan enjoyed?

Correct Answer: 2nd

Explanation

Fasting the month of Ramadan was made obligatory  during the month of Sha'ban , in the second year of Hijra in 624 AD.

31

Before the fasting of Ramadan become obligatory,Muslims were order to fast on which day?

Correct Answer: 10 Muharram

Explanation

10 Muharram referred to as the Day of Ashurah , the day on which Hussain ibn Ali was martyred in the Battle of Karbala.

The author of “Al-Akham Al-Sultania” is ____?

Correct Answer: Al-Marwadi
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Mawardi was one of the most famous thinkers in political science in the middle Ages. He was also a great sociologist.
Ambassador of the Abbasid Caliph to several important and powerful Muslim states.
34

The famous Islamic book ” Tafsir al-Tabari ” was written by _____?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari R.A
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari R.A was an influential Iranian scholar.
Tabari was some fifty years old when al-Mu'tadid became caliph
35

In which Holy Book of Non-Muslims the Quran mentioned repeatedly?

Correct Answer: Holy book of Sikh
Show explanation
Explanation

The teachings of the Sikh religion were passed down from Guru to Guru and then written down in a very special book, the Guru Granth Sahib.
36

After the death of Jafar ibn Abi Talib (رضہ), the leader of the Muslim army was _________ in the battle of Mota.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah Bin Rawaha (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

Abdullah ibn Rawaha ibn Tha'labah was one of the companions of the Islamic prophet Muhammad who was martyred in the Battle of Mu'tah.
37

Hazrat Isa (AS) prayed to Allah to become the Ummati of which Prophet?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ
Show explanation
Explanation

Injeel book was revealed on Hazrat Esa As
38

Hajjaj bin Yousuf was ___?

Correct Answer: Thaqafi
Show explanation
Explanation

Hijaj bin Yousuf was governor was iraq
He appointed Muhammad ibn al-Qasim al-Thaqafi to lead the conquest of northwestern India.
39

Hazrat Bibi Zainab (رضہ) was the daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and _____?

Correct Answer: Khadija bint Khuwaylid
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Zainab was the first and youngest daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

She married her maternal cousin, Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi'
She had two children "Ali and Umamah"

She died at the age of  30 (AH 7)and was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.


Additional Information:


Hazrat Khadija was the first wife of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ 

At the time of the marriage, Hazrat Khadijah was 40 years old. Muhammad was 25 years old.

All of the children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim

Explanation

What does Eid-ul-Fitr mean?

Explanation

Eid al-Fitr also called the "Festival of Breaking the Fast", is a religious holiday celebrated by Muslims worldwide that marks the end of the month of Ramzan

44

______is the person who starts prayer with Immam?

Correct Answer: Madurak
Show explanation
Explanation

Munfarid is the person who performs prayer alone.
Madurak is the person who starts prayer with Immam
Musbaq is the person who comes after one rakat

Correct Answer: Yatazeroon

Wa Mamin Da’abat is 12th para.
Wa Ma Ubrioo is 13th para.
Rubama is 14th Para.

Correct Answer: A’man Khalaq

Explanation

Wa Qalallazina is 19th Para.
Utlu Ma Oohi is 21st Para.

47

Name the term whose avoidance is rewarding and although doing it, is not a sin, yet it is a sort of vice?

Correct Answer: Makruh-i-Tanzihi
Show explanation
Explanation

In Sijdah or Rukooh, to say the Tasbeeh less than three times is Makrooh-e-Tanzihi.

Name the term whose avoidance is rewarding and although doing it, is not a sin, yet it is a sort of vice?

اس اصطلاح کا نام دیں جس سے اجتناب ثمر آور ہو اور اگرچہ یہ کرنا گناہ نہیں ہے ، پھر بھی یہ ایک طرح کا نائب ہے؟

Correct Answer: 2

Explanation

1 Mujadala 
2 Momenoon
49

Soam is prohibited at the time of ______?

Correct Answer: Both a & b
Show explanation
Explanation

It is also haraam to fast on the days of al-Tashreeq which are the three days after Eid al-Adha (the 11th, 12th and 13th of Dhu'l-Hijjah)
50

Hazrat Ibn Abbas (رضہ) had the title of ____?

Correct Answer: Tarjuman-ul-Quran,
Show explanation
Explanation

Ibn Abbas, was the son of Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib, an uncle of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.
51

The reward of obligatory deeds in Ramadan is multiplied by how much?

Correct Answer: 70
Show explanation
Explanation

The reward for fasting is mentioned in the following Hadeeths: “Every action of the son of Adam is given manifold reward, each good deed receiving ten times its like, up to seven hundred times.


52

what is the first prayer of the day called?

Correct Answer: Fajr
Show explanation
Explanation

During the Muslim holy month of Ramadan the start of Fajr prayer time marks the beginning of the obligatory daily fasting.

53

In which Surah the name of Allah is repeated in every verse?

Correct Answer: Surah Mujadala
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Mujadilah is the 58th chapter (sūrah) of the Quran, comprising 22 verses

Revealed in Medina,

The chapter first addresses the legality of the pre-Islamic method of divorce called zihar.

The Surah that mentions Allah in every verse is Surah Al-Mujadala.
Mujadala, meaning The Dispute.
The chapter ends by contrasting what it calls "the confederates of God" and "the confederates of Satan"



54

when the first Sariya was fought in Rabakh in____?

Correct Answer: 1. A.H
Show explanation
Explanation

A Ghazwa is a battle the Messenger of Allah ﷺ assists himself.

A sariya: is a battle where the Messenger of Allah  only sent his sahaba

Total number of Sarias is 53 or 56.

Prophet was the commander in the expedition of Tabuk.

First Islamic Non Arab was battle of Mautta 8.

55

Darul Uloom Deoband located in _____?

Correct Answer: India
Show explanation
Explanation

About Deoband Madrasah


Founded: 31 May 1866

Founder: Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi

First Principal: Maulana Muhammad Qasim

Location: Saharanpur, Uttar Pardesh

 

56

What is sighted to indicate the start of Ramadan?

Correct Answer: Moon
Show explanation
Explanation

The Moon is Earth's only proper natural satellite. 

57

What is the meal called before the beginning of fast?

Correct Answer: Suhoor
Show explanation
Explanation

Suhoor is a meal taken just before sunrise, before the day of fasting starts.

58

Kufa was made capital in which Hijra year?

Correct Answer: 36
Show explanation
Explanation

In 749, the Abbasids under al-Hasan ibn Qahtaba took Kufa and made it their capital.
59

What Is the Meaning of Astghafar?

Correct Answer: Penitence
Show explanation
Explanation

Astaghfirullah literally translates to "I seek forgiveness in God".

60

Who destroyed the idol, Sawa?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar Ibn Al As R.A
Show explanation
Explanation

Idol Sawa was destroyed by Hazrat Umar Ibn Al As R.A.
61

Which city is called the city of conference ?

Correct Answer: Geneva
Show explanation
Explanation
Several international or multinational conferences have been called the Geneva Conference, because they were held in the city of Geneva, Switzerland.
62

How many days do Muslim fast in Ramadan?

Correct Answer: 30
Show explanation
Explanation

Pillars of Islam, Arabic Arkān al-Islām, the five duties incumbent on every Muslim: shahādah, ṣalāt, zakāt, fasting during the month of Ramadan; and hajj.

63

What is the meal called that taken to break your fast in Ramadan?

Correct Answer: Iftar
Show explanation
Explanation

Iftar, also known as ftoor, is the evening meal with which Muslims end their daily Ramadan fast at sunset.

64

Which holiday mark the end of Ramadan?

Correct Answer: Eidulfitar
Show explanation
Explanation

Eid al-Fitr marks the end of Ramadan, the Muslim holy month of fasting, and is celebrated during the first three days of Shawwal.

65

Where is the grave of Hazrat Yaqub (AS)?

Correct Answer: Palestine
Show explanation
Explanation

Muslims hold that Jacob was the son of Isaac and that he preached the Oneness of God throughout his life.
66

The flesh of the slaughtered animals is divided into three portions. One third is for one’s own self. Another third for the poor and the needy and the last third is for__________?

Correct Answer: Relatives
Show explanation
Explanation

 قربانی کا گوشت تین (3) حصوں میں تقسیم کیا جائے۔

 دو  حصے صدقہ کرنا اور ایک حصہ ذاتی استعمال کے لیے بچانا مستحب اور افضل عمل ہے۔

تاہم بوقتِ ضرورت ذاتی استعمال کے لیے گھر میں ایک حصہ سے زائد بھی رکھا جاسکتا ہے۔
67

What is name of 25th Para of Holy Qur’an?

Correct Answer: Elahe Yuruddo
Show explanation
Explanation


Quran Para No 25 is Elahe Yuruddo (إِلَيْهِ يُرَدُّ)
68

What is name of 18th Para of the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Qadd Aflaha
Show explanation
Explanation

The Eighteenth Para of Quran is Qadd Aflaha (أَفْلَحَ قَدْ) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah Al Muminoon, Al-Noor, and Surah Al-Furqan.
69

In which battle Hazrat Amar bin Yasar (رضہ) was martyred?

Correct Answer: Battle of Siffin
Show explanation
Explanation

Ammar ibn Yasir
Died 657 CE (age 87)
Burial Place Raqqa, Syria

Cause of Death Martyrdom in the Battle of Siffin, Al-Sham

Cause of Death Martyrdom in the Battle of Siffin, Al-Sham
70

Where did the Prophet (ﷺ) and Hazrat Abu Bakr stay when they first left Makkah for migration?

Correct Answer: Jabal e Thawr
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Abu Bakr hid inside a cave in the Mount of Thawr.

There they stayed for three days and three nights.
71

In which Surah story of Qaroon is mentioned?

Correct Answer: Sura Qasas
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Qasas is the 28th chapter of the Qur'an with 88 verses. Lexically, qasas means to relate events in their proper sequence.
72

Hazrat Bibi Ruqayyah (R.A) was the daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and _______?

Correct Answer: Khadija bint Khuwaylid
Show explanation
Explanation


Hazrat Ruqayyah was the Second daughter and third child of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ 

She was the daughter of the Holy Prophet ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

She married (1st time) Utbah ibn Abi Lahab, but the marriage was never consummated.

She married (2nd time) to the third caliph Uthman

She had only one son (Abd Allah) but died at the age of six


Additional Information:

Hazrat Khadija was the first wife of Hazrat Muhammad 

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).
73

The ninth wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ was ____.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Safiya (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

Safiyyah bint Huyayy  was one of 9th wife of the Prophetﷺ

She was 17 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was 60 years ago

She was the daughter of Abu Sufyan 

After  Prophetﷺ death, she became involved in the power politics of the early Muslim community.


Additional Information:

Nabi e Akram (ﷺ) had eleven (11) wives also known as “Azwaaj e Mutahharaat”

74

Where is the grave of Hazrat Ayuob (AS)?

Correct Answer: Amman
Show explanation
Explanation


The Tomb of Job is one of the alleged burial sites of Job. It is located in the hills overlooking the city of Salalah in Oman's Dhofar region

Amman is the Capital of Jordan
75

Who established the foundation of Umayyad dynasty?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ameer Muawiya RA
Show explanation
Explanation

The Umayyads were the first Muslim dynasty, established in 661 in Damascus. Their dynasty succeeded the leadership of the first four caliphs—Abū Bakr, ʿUmar I, ʿUthmān, and ʿAlī. It was established by Muʿāwiyah ibn Abī Sufyān, a native of Mecca and a contemporary of the Prophet Muḥammad.
76

what do you understand by Al Salasa?

Correct Answer: Tuesday
Show explanation
Explanation

Days name in Arabic English Urdu

English Name Arabic Name Transcription
Sunday يَوم الأحَد yoom alaHad
Monday يَوم الإثنين yoom alithneen
Tuesday يَوم الثلاثاء yoom atthhulatha'
Wednesday يَوم الأربعاء yoom alarbi3a'
Thursday يَوم الخميس yoom alkhamees
Friday يَوم الجمعة yoom aljuma3a
Saturday يَوم السبت yoom assabt
77

Khalid ibn al-Walid (R.A) embraced Islam in which Hijri?

Correct Answer: 8th Hijri
Show explanation
Explanation

Khalid ibn al-Walid was a well-known Arab Muslim commander in the service of the Islamic prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and the caliphs Abu Bakr (R.A) and Umar (R.A). Historians have reported different narrations on the date that Khalid b. Walid converted to Islam. In the year 6 AH or 8 AH Khalid embraced Islam in Muhammad’s presence alongside the Qurayshite Amr ibn al-As. However, according to a famous report, Khalid converted to Islam in the first of Safar 8 AH (c. 629)before the Conquest of Mecca.
78

Who will be first person questioned on Day of Qiyamat?

Correct Answer: Jibreel (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation

Jibreel (A.S) who acts as intermediary between God and humans and as bearer of revelation to the prophets, most notably to Muhammad.

79

Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) is buried in_____?

Correct Answer: Jerusalem
Show explanation
Explanation

important question : 

Namrud ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into the fire

Nimrod, the king of Babylon, commanded his men to throw Prophet Ibrahim (AS) into a huge pit of burning fire

Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days

Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)
Hazrat Ibrahim is buried in Jerusalem, Israel. 

Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq

Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

Abraham had two children,

1.      Isaac

2.      Ismael,

****
80

Battle of Qadisiya was fought against the Persians in ________A.D.

Correct Answer: 636 AD
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of al-Qadisiyyah, fought in 636, was a decisive battle between the Arab Muslim army and the army of the Sasanian Empire of Persia during the first period of Muslim conquests.
81

The first meal in Jannah will be_______?

Correct Answer: Fish Liver
Show explanation
Explanation

It is proven in the saheeh Sunnah that the first food offered to welcome the people of Paradise when they enter it will be “the caudate lobe of whale liver”.
82

Tafseer al Quran al Azeem was written by ______?

Correct Answer: Ismail bin Kathir (Ibn Al Athir)
Show explanation
Explanation

 Imam Ibn Kathir on Tawassul: Many have stated this tradition. One of them is Abū Mansūr Sabbagh who writes in his book al-Hikayat-ul-mashhurah that, according to ‘Utbi, once he was sitting beside the Prophet’s grave when a bedouin came and he said, “Peace be on you, O Allah’s Messenger.
83

Takbeer-e-Tashreeq is recited in______?

Correct Answer: Eid-ul-Uzha
Show explanation
Explanation

Takbir al-Tashreeq is a prayer recited by pilgrims after every Fardh Salah during Hajj.

Starting immediately after Fajr Salah on the 9th of Dhul Hijjah, it is Wajib for Hajj pilgrims to audibly recite Takbir al-Tashreeq at least once after every Fardh Salah, up to and including Asr Salah on the 13th of Dhul Hijjah (a total of 23 Fardh Salahs).

 

اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ لَا إلَهَ إلَّا اللَّهُ وَاَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْد

 
84

The period of the reign of Abu Ishaq Muḥammad ibn Harun al-Rashid is from_____?

Correct Answer: 796-842
Show explanation
Explanation

Abū Isḥāq Muḥammad ibn Hārūn al-Rashīd, better known by his regnal name al-Muʿtaṣim biʾllāh , was the eighth Abbasid caliph, ruling from 833 until his death in 842.
85

In which Surah every verse ends with the letter ‘Ra’?

Correct Answer: Kausar
Show explanation
Explanation


Surah Al-'Asr also ends 'Ra'.


86

How many times Bismillahir Rahmaanir Raheem is repeated in the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: 114
Show explanation
Explanation
Bismillahir Rahmaanir Raheem is repeated in the Holy Quran  114 Times
Every surah in the Quran opens with the statement as the Bismillah, except Surah 9 (Surah Tuba).

Surah Naml, which is the 27th Chapter, has the entire bismillahir rahmanir raheem in the 30th verse.
This is why Surah Naml is known as the Surah with two bismillahs.
87

Who are exempt from Ramadan’s fasting?

Correct Answer: All of these
Show explanation
Explanation

As one of the five pillars, or duties, of Islam, fasting during the month of Ramadan is mandatory for all healthy adult Muslims. Children who have not reached puberty, the elderly, those who are physically or mentally incapable of fasting, pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers and travelers are exempt.
88

Where is the grave of Hazrat Zikriya (A.S)?

Correct Answer: Syria
Show explanation
Explanation

Syria, officially the Syrian Arab Republic, is a country in Western Asia, bordering Lebanon to the southwest, the Mediterranean Sea to the west, Turkey to the north, Iraq to the east, Jordan to the south, and Israel to the southwest.

89

Which days are called Ayam-e-Tashreeq?

Correct Answer: 11, 12 and 13 Zil Haj
Show explanation
Explanation

Uqbah bin Amir (R) said: The Messenger of Allahﷺsaid:

"The Day of Arafah, the Day of Nahr, and the Days of Tashriq (11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul-Hijjah) are Eid for us. The people of Islam, and they are days of eating and drinking,"

Jami at-Tirmidhi 773, Sunan Ibn Majah Vol 1: 1720 and Sunan an Nasai 3007
90

What is the name of the forces of Hell (Jahanam)?

Correct Answer: Zabaniyah
Show explanation
Explanation

In Islam the Zabaniyah (الزبانية‎) are the forces of hell, who torment the sinners, also called the Angels of punishment or Guardians of Hell.

They are often identified with the Nineteen Angels of Hell mentioned in Quran 66:6 and 74:30 or as their subordinates.

Malik is the name of the angel of Jahanam [Hell]
91

Ghazwa _____ was fought 3 times.

Correct Answer: Ghazwa Badr
Show explanation
Explanation

Badr is a town in Al Madinah Province, Al-Hijaz, Saudi Arabia. It is located about 130 km from the Islamic holy city of Medina. It was the location of the Battle of Badr, between the Quraishi-led Polytheists, and the Muslims under the leadership of Muhammad, in 624 CE.
92

Which is the oldest and the first Islamic cemetery in the World?

Correct Answer: Jannat al-Baqīʿ
Show explanation
Explanation

Jannat al-Baqīʿ is the oldest and the first Islamic cemetery of Medina in the Hejazi region of present-day Saudi Arabia. It is located to the southeast of the Prophet's Mosque, which contains the graves of some of Prophet Muhammad's family and friends. It is also known as Baqīʿ al-Gharqad.

93

“Mahram-e-Raza-e-Nabuwat” is the title of _________?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Huzefa bin -al-Yaman (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

 Hazrat Abu Huzaifa (R.A) was amongst the blessed personalities who had embraced Islam in its early days. He (R.A) was the son of Utbah bin Rabiah,

During the Caliphate of Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddique (R.A), Hazrat Abu Huzaifa (R.A) took part in a battle against the impostor, Musailma Kazzab (the impostor who claimed to be a Prophet), thus fulfilled his desire of becoming Shaheed.
94

Kitab-ul-Shamayel relates to ___________?

Correct Answer: Life of the Holy Prophet ﷺ
Show explanation
Explanation

Kitab-ul-Shamayel to the Life of the Holy Prophet ﷺ,

Kitab-ul-Shamayel was written by Imam Tirmizi
95

The study of Religions is called:________?

Correct Answer: Theology
Show explanation
Explanation

Theology is the study of religion. It examines the human experience of faith, and how different people and cultures express it. Theologians have the complex job of thinking about and debating the nature of God. Studying theology means taking on challenging questions about the meaning of religion.
96

Hazrat Khadija (RA) was the ______ person to convert to Islam.

Correct Answer: First
Show explanation
Explanation

Not long after, Abu Bakr accepted Islam and was the first person outside the family of Muhammad to openly become a Muslim. He was instrumental in the conversion of many people to the Islamic faith and early in 623, Abu Bakr's daughter Aisha was married to Muhammad, strengthening the ties between the two men.

First women Hazrat Khadija who accept Islam.

97

What is name of 23rd Para of Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Wa Mali
Show explanation
Explanation

4 surah’s in 23rd Para
Quran Para 23, (also referred to as Juz 23) spans across four surahs.
98

How many years did Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ preach Islam during his lifetime?

Correct Answer: 23
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammad, the final Islamic prophet, was born and lived in Mecca for the first 52 years of his life (570–622 CE) until the Hijra. This period of his life is characterized by his proclamation of prophet hood.
99

Who was the second son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Abdullah was the Second son of Hazrat Muhammad and Hazrat Khadijja

He has died at the age of 4

He was buried at Jannat al-Mualla Mecca, Arabia


Additional Information:

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).
100

Which Sura is the name of one metal?

Correct Answer: Hadeed
Show explanation
Explanation

The Surah named after a metal is Surah Al-Hadid, which means "The Iron"

It is the 57th chapter of the Quran

It is known for the word "Iron" (Al-Hadid) appearing in its 25th verse

Classification: Medinan

Position Juz: 27

No. of verses: 29

No. of Rukus: 4

No. of words: 575

No. of letters: 2475

101

Kalima Tayyaba, salat, Zakat, Hajj and soam are the fundamental ________ of Islam?

Correct Answer: Pillars
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah al-Ḥadith  is the 57th chapter of the Quran with 29 verses. The chapter takes its name from that word which appears in the 25th verse.
102

What is the name of 15th Para of Holy Qur’an?

Correct Answer: Subhanallazi
Show explanation
Explanation

The fifteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Subhanallazi (الَّذِي سُبْحَانَ) which has 2 Surahs that are Surah Al Isra and Surah Al-Kahf. 16. The Sixteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Qal Alam (أَلَمْ قَالَ) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah Al-Kahf, Surah Maryam and Surah Taha. 17.
103

What was the age of Hazrat Aisha (R.A) when Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ died?

Correct Answer: 18 years
Show explanation
Explanation
Hazrat Aisha (R.A) was 18 years old when Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) died 

Hazrat Ayesha was the 3rd wife and most learned of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths

Muhammad married Hazrat Ayesha when he was 53 years old and she was only six years old.

He had intercourse with Hazrat Ayesha when she was nine years old.

Hazrat Ayesha was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker 

She gives the title of Siddiqa, meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

She Died at the age of 67 in Medina 

She was  buried in Jannat al-Baqi

104

_______ was the only Ummul Momineen who was not buried in Jannatul Baqi.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Khadija (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, was the first wife and first follower of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

Hazrat Khadija (RA) was buried in Makkah.

Khadijah died in "Ramadan of the year 10 after the Prophethood", i.e., in November AD 619. Muhammad later called this tenth year "the Year of Sorrow", as his uncle and protector Abu Talib also died at this time.
105

What was the language of Arab people?

Correct Answer: Arabic
Show explanation
Explanation

Arabic

Native speakers 292 million (2017)
Language family Afro-Asiatic Semitic West Semitic Central Semitic Arabic
Standard forms Modern Standard Arabic
106

Kitab-ul-Shamayel was written by _____?

Correct Answer: Imam Tirmizi
Show explanation
Explanation

Abu ʿIsa Muḥammad ibn ʿIsa as-Sulami aḍ-Ḍarīr al-Bughī at-Tirmidhī, often referred to as Imām al-Termezī, was a Persian Islamic scholar, and collector of hadith from Termez. He wrote al-Jami` as-Sahih, one of the six canonical hadith compilations in Sunni Islam.
107

It is said that Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was from the city of ‘Ur’. It is located in______?

Correct Answer: Mesopotamia
Show explanation
Explanation

Mesopotamia is a historical region of Western Asia situated within the Tigris–Euphrates river system, in the northern part of the Fertile Crescent.
108

Name the Prophet who was the first man to write?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Idrees (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation

Enoch is Idris the prophet. He was the first of Adam's children to be given prophecy and the first to write with a pen. Jared lived 800 years after the birth of Enoch, and had more sons and daughters. So all of the years lived by Jared were 962, then he died.
109

Abu Ubaidah bin Jarrah was the conqueror of _______?

Correct Answer: Yarmuk
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Yarmouk was a major battle between the army of the Byzantine Empire and the Muslim forces of the Rashidun Caliphate.

110

The Battle of Sallasil or the Battle of Chains was fought between_______and the Muslims?

Correct Answer: Persians
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Sallasil or the Battle of Chains was the first battle fought between the Rashidun Caliphate and the Sasanian Persian Empire.
111

Who discovered the “Zam Zam Well”?

Correct Answer: Prophet Ismail
Show explanation
Explanation

Zam Zam water was discovered by Prophet Ismail (peace be upon him) and his mother Hajira (may Allah be pleased with her). The well is located approximately 20 meters east of the Kaaba and Zam Zam well is 35 meters deep.
112

Hazrat Yousaf (AS) was put into the jail by the king of Egypt. He remained behind the bars for

Correct Answer: 5 years
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Yousaf (AS) was put into the jail by the king of Egypt. He remained behind the bars for  5 years.

Hazrat Yousaf (AS) remained in the well for 3 DAYS.

113

Islam✅

930 years✅

Hazrat Adam (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

1st✅

How many sons did Hazrat Adam (A.S) have?

3✅

Where is the grave of Hazrat Adam (علیہ)?

Sri Lanka ✅

ND5.3.2023
115

Age of Hazrat Adam (A.S) at Sheesh’s (A.S) birth was _____________years?

Correct Answer: 130
Show explanation
Explanation

Adam was the first Muslim, as the Quran, states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

Known for: First human being

Spouse(s): Hawwā (حواء)

The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 Feet

Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years of age when Shees alaihissalam was born

The language is spoken by Adam in the Garden of Eden.

What was the Religion of Hazrat Adam?

Islam✅

Hazrat Adam's (AS) total age ?

930 years✅

Hazrat Adam (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

1st✅

How many sons did Hazrat Adam (A.S) have?

3✅

Where is the grave of Hazrat Adam (علیہ)?

Sri Lanka ✅

ND5.3.2023
116

Who was second Prophet after Hazrat Adam a.s?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Shees a.s
Show explanation
Explanation

Seesh (Seeth) means a gift.

Prophet Shees (Seth) Alaihissalam was the third son of Prophet Adam Alaihissalam

Adam alaihissalam was 130 years of age when Shees alaihissalam was born. 

Prophet Shees alaihissalam is referenced in the Holy Quran, but not by name.

However, He is mentioned in hadeeth

ND5.3.2023



117

حضرت زید بن حارث کا نام کس سورہ میں آیا ہے؟

Correct Answer: سورۃ الاحزاب
Show explanation
Explanation

ابو اسامہ زید بن حارثہ بن شراحیل (شراحبیل) کلبی (متوفی 8 ھ) رسول خدا صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ و سلم کے آزاد کردہ غلام اور منھ بولے بیٹے ہیں۔

سب سے پہلے ایمان لانے والے مسلمانوں میں سے ہیں اور

ایسے اکلوتے صحابی ہیں کہ جن کا نام قرآن مجید میں ذکر ہوا ہے

زید بن حارث رضی اللہ تعالی عنہ کا ذکر قرآن مجید کی سورہ احزاب کی آیت نمبر 37 میں ہے

ND5.3.2023

118

قرآن حکیم نے کس فعل کو قتل سے بھی زیادہ شدید قرار دیا ہے؟

Correct Answer: فتنہ و فساد
Show explanation
Explanation

قرآن کی آیت مبارکہ ہے کہ”فتنہ قتل سے بھی بْرا عمل ہے“

(البقرہ-191)

ND5.3.2023

119

حضور کریم کی ولادت واقعہ الفیل کے کتنے روز بعد ہوئی؟

Correct Answer: 45 روز بعد
Show explanation
Explanation

عام الفیل کے پچاس روز بعد والد گرامی کے وصال کے بعد رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ و آلہ وسلم ماہ ربیع الاوّل کی بارہ تاریخ کو پیدا ہوئے۔

عام الفیل واقعہ 570ء میں پیش آیا

حضوراکرم صلی اللہ تعالیٰ علیہ وسلم کی پیدائش سے صرف پچپن دن پہلے یمن کا بادشاہ ”ابرہہ” ہاتھیوں کی فوج لے کر کعبہ ڈھانے کے لئے مکہ پر حملہ آور ہوا تھا۔
ND5.3.2023
120

Ghaseel ul Malaika is the title of_____?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Hanzala (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hanzala earned the title of Ghaseel al-Malāʾika or the one cleansed by the angels

Hanzala Ibn Abi Amir belonged to the Banu Aus tribe of the Ansar.

Hanzala was just 24 years old when he died in the Battle of Uhud 

Hanzala was one of the companions of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

***

agm.5.3.23

ND17-7-2023
121

No action in Islam is accepted without:

Correct Answer: Tauheed
Show explanation
Explanation

Tawhid is the indivisible oneness concept of monotheism in Islam. Tawhid is the religion's central and single most important concept, upon which a Muslim's entire religious adherence rests. It unequivocally holds that God as per Islam is One and Single.

122

Which is the most liked place by Allah Ta’ala?

Correct Answer: Mosque
Show explanation
Explanation

A mosque is a place of worship for Muslims.

123

What is name of 14th Para of Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Rubama
Show explanation
Explanation

Total Para of Quran = 30
Total Surah in Quran = 114
Total Sajda in Quran = 7
124

Surah Tauba the only Surah Which does not start with Bismillah is in Which Parah?

Correct Answer: 10 & 11
Show explanation
Explanation

At-Tawbah  meaning: The Repentance also known as Bara'ah , is the ninth chapter (sūrah) of the Quran. It is the only Surah of the Quran that does not begin with Bismillah.

125

Where is Hazrat Abdul Muttalib buried?

Correct Answer: Makkah
Show explanation
Explanation

Abd al-Muttalib   
Died 578 (aged 80–81) Mecca, Hejaz (present-day Saudi Arabia)
Burial Jannat al-Mu'alla

126

Ohad is located near _______?

Correct Answer: Madina
Show explanation
Explanation

Ohad is located near  to Madina. It is 1,077 m (3,533 ft) high and 7.5 km long.
Province:  Al Madinah Region
Location:  Medina, Saudi Arabia
127

Hazrat Bibi Umm Kulthum (R.A) was the daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and _______?

Correct Answer: Khadija bint Khuwaylid
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Umm-e-Kalthum was 3rd daughter and 4th child of the Holy Prophet ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

After the death of her sister Ruqayya left Uthman a widower, he married Umm Kulthum

Umm Kulthum died in 630 (8 AH) at age of 27

She was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.


Additional Information:


Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).



128

What is the name of 26th Para of the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Ha’a Meem,
Show explanation
Explanation

There are total 30 para in Holy Quran,

 27th para ""Qala Fama Khatbukum""

28th para ""Qad samiʿa 'llāh

*****

The twenty-six Para or Juz of the Quran is Ha'a Meem (حم) which has 5 Surahs from Surah Ahqaf to Surah qaaf

129

Who was the pupils of Imam Abu Hanifa?

Correct Answer: Both
Show explanation
Explanation

Imam Abu Hanifa born kufa  in 699CE ,80 hijri

Died: 767 CE ,150 AH

Buried : Baghdad Iraq


130

Imam Abu Hanifa died in _________AD?

Correct Answer: 767AD

Explanation

Imam Abu Hanifa born kufa  in 699CE ,80 hijri

Died: 767 CE ,150 AH

Buried: Baghdad Iraq

131

When did Imam Abu Hanifa die according to the Hijri calendar?

Correct Answer: 150 Hijri
Show explanation
Explanation
  1. Imam Abu Hanifa Born: 699 (80 Hijri); Kufa, Umayyad Caliphate
  2. Imam Abu Hanifa Died: 767 (150 Hijri); Baghdad, Abbasid Calip
  3. Imam Abu Hanifa Full name: Numan ibn Thābit ibn Zūṭā ibn Marzubān
  4. Imam Abu Hanifa Place of burial: Abu Hanifa Mosque, Baghdad, Iraq
  6. Imam Abu Hanifa Children: Hammad ibn Abi Hanifah.
132

When was Imam Abu Hanifa born?

Correct Answer: 80 Hijri
Show explanation
Explanation
  1. Imam Abu Hanifa Born: 699 (80 Hijri); Kufa, Umayyad Caliphate
  2. Imam Abu Hanifa Died: 767 (150 Hijri); Baghdad, Abbasid Calip
  3. Imam Abu Hanifa Full name: Numan ibn Thābit ibn Zūṭā ibn Marzubān
  4. Imam Abu Hanifa Place of burial: Abu Hanifa Mosque, Baghdad, Iraq
  6. Imam Abu Hanifa Children: Hammad ibn Abi Hanifah.

Heart of a Muslim becomes black as mentioned in Hadith Nabvee when:

Correct Answer: He leaves three timed consecutively Juma prayer
Show explanation
Explanation

As mentioned in Hadith Nabvi when a Muslim leaves three timed consecutively Juma prayer his Heart becomes black 

134

Salat to supplicate for rain is called:

Correct Answer: Estisqaa
Show explanation
Explanation

ð  People offered special prayers (Namaz-e-Istisqa) for rain at local mosques

ð  Namaz-e-Khasoof (نماز خسوف) is offered at the time of Lunar eclipse

ð  Namaz-e-Kasoof (نماز کسوف) is offered at the time of Solar eclipse

135

Hazrat Abdullah was the son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and ______.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Khadija
Show explanation
Explanation

 Hazrat Abdullah was the son of Hazrat Muhammad and Hazrat Khadijja

He was the Second son of Hazrat Muhammad

He has died at the age of 4

He was buried at Jannat al-Mualla Mecca, Arabia

All children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).


Additional Information:

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 




136

The Ridda wars were fought by which Caliph?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

The Ridda Wars or Wars of Apostasy, were a series of military campaigns launched by the Caliph Abu Bakr against rebel Arabian tribes during 632 and 633, just after the death of the Islamic Prophet Muhammad.

137

The other name of Battle of Badr in the Quran?

Correct Answer: Yaum ul Furqan
Show explanation
Explanation

All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

Furqan the second name of the Battle of Badr

Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion. ( Yaum ul Furqan )

It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

 mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

Total: Muslim 313-317

Muhajirun: 82-85

Ansar: 231

Aws: 61

Khazraj: 170

2 horses

 

70 camels

**

138

Which month is known as Sayeed us Shahoor?

Correct Answer: Ramazan
Show explanation
Explanation

"Ramzan is 9th Islamic month,

Battle of badar fought 17th Ramadan, 2 AH,

Makkah conqured on 20th ramadan 8th Hijri."

139

What is the name of 19th Para of Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Wa Qalallazina
Show explanation
Explanation

Para of Quran Complete List
1. The first Para or Juz of the Quran is Alif-laam-meem(آلم ) Which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah Al-Fatiha and another one is Al-Baqarah.

2. The second Para or Juz of the Quran is Sayaqūlu (سَيَقُولُ) which have only 1 Surah that is Surah Al-Baqarah.

3. The third Para or Juz of the Quran is Tilka ’r-Rusulu (الرُّسُلُ تِلْكَ) which has two Surahs the first one is Surah Al-Baqarah and second is Aal-Imran

4. The fourth Para or Juz of the Quran is Lan Tana Loo (تَنَالُوا لَنْ) which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah Aal-Imran and second is An-Nisa

5. The Fifth Para or Juz of the Quran is Wal Mohsanat (وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ) which have 1 Surah that is Surah is An-Nisa

6. The Sixth Para or Juz of the Quran is La Yuhibbullah (اللَّهُ يُحِبُّ لَا) which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah An-Nisa and second is Surah Al-Maidah

7. The Seventh Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Iza Samiu (سَمِعُوا وَإِذَا) which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah Al-Maidah and second is Surah Al-Anaam

8. The eighth Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Lau Annana (أَنَّنَا وَلَوْ) which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah Al-Anaam and second is Surah Al-Araf.

9. The Ninth Para or Juz of the Quran is Qalal Malao (الْمَلَأُ قَالَ) which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah Al-Araf and second is Surah Al Anfal.

10. The tenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa A'lamu (وَاعْلَمُوا) which has 2 Surahs the first one is Surah Al- Anfal and second is Surah At-taubah.

11. The eleventh Para or Juz of the Quran is Yatazeroon (يَعْتَذِرُونَ) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah At-taubah, Surah Younus and Surah Hood

12. The twelfth Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Mamin Da'abat (دَابَّةٍ مِنْ وَمَا) which has 2 Surahs that are Surah Hood and Surah Yusuf

13. The thirteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Ma Ubrioo (بَرِّئُوَمَا) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah Yusuf, Surah Raad, and Surah Ibrahim

14. The fourteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Rubama (رُبَمَا) which has 2 Surahs that are Surah Hijr and Surah Nahl

15. The fifteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Subhanallazi (الَّذِي سُبْحَانَ) which has 2 Surahs that are Surah Al Isra and Surah Al-Kahf

16. The Sixteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Qal Alam (أَلَمْ قَالَ) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah Al-Kahf, Surah Maryam and Surah Taha

17. The Seventeenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Aqtarabo (اقْتَرَبَ) which has 2 Surahs that are Surah Al Anbiya and Surah Al-Hajj

18. The Eighteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Qadd Aflaha (أَفْلَحَ قَدْ) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah Al Muminoon, Al-Noor, and Surah Al-Furqan

19. The Nineteenth Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Qalallazina (الَّذِينَ وَقَالَ) which has 3 Surahs that are Surah Al Furqan, Al-Shu’ara, and Surah Al Namal.

20. The twentieth Para or Juz of the Quran is A'man Khalaq (خَلَقَ أَمَّنْ) which has 3 Surahs that Surah Al Namal, Al-Qasas, and Surah Al-Ankaboot

21. The twenty-one Para or Juz of the Quran is Utlu Ma Oohi (أُوحِيَ مَا اتْلُ) which has 4 Surahs from Surah Ar-Room to Surah Al-Ahzab

22. The twenty-two Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Manyaqnut (يَقْنُتْ وَمَنْ) which has 4 Surahs from Surah Al Ahzab to Surah Yaseen

23. The twenty-three Para or Juz of the Quran is Wa Mali (لِيَ وَمَا) which has 4 Surahs from Surah Yaseen to Surah Al-Zumar

24. The twenty-four Para or Juz of the Quran is Faman Azlam (أَظْلَمُ فَمَنْ) which has 3 Surahs from Surah Al-Zumar to Surah Fussilat

25. The twenty-five Para or Juz of the Quran is Elahe Yuruddo (يُرَدُّ إِلَيْهِ) which has 5 Surahs from Surah Fussilat to Surah Al-Jathiya

26. The twenty-six Para or Juz of the Quran is Ha'a Meem (حم) which has 5 Surahs from Surah Ahqaf to Surah qaaf

27. The twenty-seven Para or Juz of the Quran is Qala Fama Khatbukum (خَطْبُكُمْ فَمَا قَال) which has 7 Surahs from Surah Dhariyaat to Surah Al-Hadid

28. The twenty-eight Para or Juz of the Quran is Qadd Sami Allah (اللَّهُ سَمِعَ قَدْ) which has 9 from Surah Mujadilah to Surah Al-Tahrim

29. The twenty-nine Para or Juz of the Quran is Tabarakallazi (الَّذِي تَبَارَكَ) which has 11 Surahs from Surah Al-Mulk to Surah Al-Mursalat

30. The thirty Para or Juz of the Quran is Amma Yatasa'aloon (يَتَسَاءَلُونَ عَمَّ) which has 37 Surahs from Surah An Naba to Surah An-Nas

140

How many Ashra are there in Ramzan?

Correct Answer: All of these
Show explanation
Explanation

There are the Three Ashras of Ramadan

"First Ashra of Ramadan is "Days of mercy" (Rehmat),

2nd is "days of forgiveness" (Maghfirayat),

3rd is "days of refuge from hell fire"(Jahanum ke aag sa nijahat) "

141

Allah forgives _____ Muslims every day at the time of iftar in Ramadan?

Correct Answer: 10 lakh
Show explanation
Explanation

Ramzan is 9th Islamic month

Roza Farz on 2nd hijri,

Fasting in the month of Ramadan is compulsory on every Muslim adult. 

142

Animals are routinely sacrificed on Eid ul Adha in memory of the sacrifice of _________?

Correct Answer: Prophet Abraham (A.S) and Prophet Ishmael (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation

The story of Qurbani begins before Islam, with the Prophet Ibrahim (as) and his son Ismail (as). It reminds us of their strength of faith, and of God’s favour and mercy upon the believers.

Eid al-Adha is known as Festival of the Sacrifice in Islamic faith and Muslims worldwide. Eid-ul-Adha observes every year in the month of Islamic Date of ZULHIJJAH 10."

143

The basic objectives of paying Zakat is

Correct Answer: To purify wealth
Show explanation
Explanation

Both Zakat and Fasting were made compulsory in the second Hijrah while Haj was made obligatory in 9th Hijrah.

144

Haq Mahar in Islam is fixed only __________ misqal.

Correct Answer: 400
Show explanation
Explanation

Haq Mahr is the obligation, in the form of money or possessions paid by the groom, to the bride at the time of islamic marriage.

145

which Prophet was raised and preach in Egypt?

Correct Answer: Musa A.S
Show explanation
Explanation

Mosa (A.S) in Judaeo-Christian theology, considered a prophet and messenger in Islam, is the most frequently mentioned individual in the Qur'an, his name being mentioned 135 times.

The Quran states that Musa was sent by Allah to the Pharaoh of Egypt and his establishments and the Israelites for guidance and warning.

146

What is Fateh -e- Mubeen meant for?

Correct Answer: Hudibiya Pact
Show explanation
Explanation

 Sulah-e-Hudaebia in 6 Hijri 

It is also known as Fateh-e-Mubeen & Baet-e-Rizwan


147

Which bird flew over the Island of Sheba (Saba) and noticed the Queen Balqis:

Correct Answer: Woodpecker
Show explanation
Explanation

The hoopoe bird appears and tells Solomon that he has been flying far and came to the land of Sheba where, he says,

“I found a woman ruling over them and she has been given of everything and she possesses a mighty throne” (Sura 27:20)

She was in the era of Prophet Suliaman

148

How many sons Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ had with Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiya (RA)?

Correct Answer: 1
Show explanation
Explanation

Only 1 son (child) was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA) 

Ibrahim was the third son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Born in Dhu al-Hijjah 8 AH 

He Died on Shawwal 29, 10 AH, and was buried
in Jannat al-Baqi, Medina


Additional Information:

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Explanation

He is the second prophet mentioned in the Quran.

Born in  Egypt.

Hazrat Idrees (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 4th heaven.

154

رزق حالل کی تالش فرضی عبادت کے بعد۔۔۔۔۔۔۔۔۔

Correct Answer: فرض ہے
Show explanation
Explanation

 جائز طریقے سے حلال روزی کمانا اور کھا نا اسی طرح فرض ہے جس طرح دوسرے ارکان دین پر عمل کرنا۔

155

Kiraman Katbeen are?

Correct Answer: In-charge of both left and right shoulders
Show explanation
Explanation

Kiramin Katbeen means Honorable writers.

Kiraman Katbeen are in-charge of both left and right shoulders.

Two angels are appointed on each person.

Both of them maintains the good deeds and the other one records evil deeds.

These two angels are named as Kiraman Katibeen (AS).

156

One who disbelieves in Zakat is a?

Correct Answer: Kafir
Show explanation
Explanation
  1. Zakat literally means Purification.
  2. Zakat is the 3rd fundamental pillar of Islam.
  3. When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H.
  4. Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) said that there was no difference between Salat and Zakat.
  5. The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.
  6. Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.
  7. Without which act the prayer is useless? Zakat
  8. One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir
  9. Person who is liable to pay Zakat is called………… Sahib-e-Nisab
  10. How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5
  11. How many goats render are to pay zakat? 40
  12. How many sheep render are to pay zakat? 40
  13. How many cows render are to pay Zakat? 30-40
  14. How many bulls render to pay Zakat? 30-40
  15. How may buffaloes render are to pay zakat? 30-40
  16. Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas
  17. Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas
  18. “Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)
  19. How many times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs? 22 times
  20. Who are not entitled to get Zakat? Parents, Husband, wife and children
  22. Zakat became compulsory during the 2nd year of hijrah but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.

A collection of two or more than 2 words are called

Correct Answer: Markab

Explanation

Islam prohibit racial

159

The companions of Prophet who receive good news of jannat on Earth is called

Correct Answer: Ashra e mubashra
Show explanation
Explanation

Ashra e mubashra 10 companions of Prophet

160

In which mosque, seventy prophets have offered prayers?

Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Kheef
Show explanation
Explanation

This mosque is the biggest Mosque of Muna and it is said that near about seventy Prophets of Allah are lying to rest (buried) here.

26 prophets are mentioned in the Qur'an, although some believe there have been 124 000.

Abdul Mutalib died in ___ A.D.

Correct Answer: 578

Abd al-Muttalib was Born 497

163

On which date throwing of stones is performed on Jumeraat?

Correct Answer: 10th Zulhajj
Show explanation
Explanation


The final ritual of the Hajj, the “Great Jamara”

Pilgrims throw seven stones at a wall representing Satan.
164

Share of daughter’(s) if both son’s & daughters exist, then

Correct Answer: 2:1
Show explanation
Explanation

SHARE OF DAUGHTER’(s)

 

IF ONLY ONE DAUGHTER (and NO Sons)  THEN

DAUGHTER = 1/2


 IF TWO OR MORE DAUGHTERS ONLY (and NO Sons) THEN

DAUGHTERS = 2/3

(to be shared equally between all of them)

 

 IF both SON’s & DAUGHTERS EXIST,  THEN

SON:DAUGHTER = 2:1


*******

FOR MORE Details ; http://www.pja.gov.pk/system/files/Inheritance.pdf

Which prophet give the good news of arrival of Muhammad (PBUH) to his people

Name the last Sariya Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqas (R.A)

The first "Sariya" (battle ) was known as "Saiful Bahar"

It is considered the touchstone of theology

Translate Surah Kausar( Either in Urdu 0r English)

Correct Answer: سورہ کوثر کا ترجمہ تفسیر میں ملاحظہ فرمائیں
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah kausar:

 English Translation: 

To thee have We granted the Fount (of Abundance). Therefore to thy Lord turn in Prayer and Sacrifice. For he who hateth thee, he will be cut off (from Future Hope)

****

Urdu Translation

لہٰذا تم اپنے پروردگار (کی خوشنودی) کے لیے نماز پڑھو، اور قربانی کرو۔ يقین جانو تمہارا دشمن ہی وہ ہے جس کی جڑ کٹی ہوئی ہے۔

173

How many Muhajar in Ghazwa e Badr

Correct Answer: 82-85
Show explanation
Explanation

All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

 mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

Total: Muslim 313-317

Muhajirun: 82-85

Ansar: 231

Aws: 61

Khazraj: 170

2 horses

70 camels

**

ND23-2-2023

174

Who raised the flag in Ghazwa for the first time?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Hamza (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib was a foster brother, and paternal uncle of the prophet Muhammadﷺ.

He was killed in the Battle of Uhud on 22 March 625 (3 Shawwal 3 hijri).
175

The Salat-e-Shukar is ______?

Correct Answer: Mustahab
Show explanation
Explanation

Salat e Shukar is Mustahab

Mustahab means favored or virtuous actions

Mustahabb actions are those whose ruling in Islamic law falls between mubah and wajib

Salat e Shukar is also known as Sajdah Shukr

Shukr prayer (Prayer of thanks) is performed in order to thank Allah (swt) 

It doesn't have a specific time.

It is at least 2 rakahs.

176

Which Alphabet is mostly used in Quran?

Correct Answer: Alif
Show explanation
Explanation

Out Of Which 5 Most Used Alphabets are: 

Alif “ا” occurs 43542 times in Quran

Lam “ل” occurs 38191 times

Nun “ن” occurs 27270

Mim “م” used 26735 times

Waw “و” which is used 24813 times in Quran.

The letter (ظ) is pronounced Dhahd (ظاء).

Which is said to be spelled 842 times in the whole book.

while 

the shortest word of the Quran is constituted of Just 1 letter and is “ن” in verse 1 of Surah Qalam 

the longest word is “فأسقيناكموه” in verse 22 of Surah Hijr

Correct Answer: Zoan (ظ)

Which is said to be spelled 842 times in the whole book.

***
Out Of Which 5 Most Used Alphabets are:

 Alif “ا” occurs 43542 times in Quran

Lam “ل” occurs 38191 times

Nun “ن” occurs 27270

Mim “م” used 26735 times

Waw “و” which is used 24813 times in Quran.

while 

the shortest word of the Quran is constituted of Just 1 letter and is “ن” in verse 1 of Surah Qalam 

the longest word is “فأسقيناكموه” in verse 22 of Surah Hijr

while

The Holy Quran was first time translated into Punjabi:

183

When Hudood Ordinance was introduced in Pakistan first time.

Correct Answer: 1979
Show explanation
Explanation

In 1979 Hudood Ordinance was introduced in Pakistan first time.

Hudood in Islam: "Hudud is an Arabic term that refers to Islamic penal law or Quranic punishments."

It is part of the military ruler Zia-ul-Haq's "Islamisation" process

On 10 February 1979, President Ziaul Haq promulgated four ordinances, collectively referred to as the “Hudood Ordinances”

Correct Answer: Hunger and Fear

a bit of fear and hunger,

and loss in wealth and lives

and fruits

and give good tidings to the patient

روزہ کس سن ہجری میں فرض ہوا؟

Correct Answer: 10th Shaban, 2 A.H

Fasting the month of Ramadan was made obligatory (wajib) during the month of Sha'ban (8th Month), in the second year after the Muslims migrated from Makkah to Medina (624 AD).

Abu Tarab is the title of ____?

187

Islamic Calendar starts from _____?

Correct Answer: Hijri
Show explanation
Explanation

Islamic lunar calendar, which begins its count from the Islamic New Year in 622 CE

 Islamic lunar calendar has only 354 or 355 days in its year."

Hijri calendar was started in16 Hijri

Islamic Calendar starts from Hijri

Islamic calendar established by: Hazrat Umar (R.A)

*****


188

The Islamic calendar is also called?

Correct Answer: All of the above
Show explanation
Explanation

Islamic calendar established by Hazrat Umar (R.A)


The first year of the Islamic calendar began in 622 CE when the Prophet Muhammad and his followers migrated from Makkah to Medina

Must remember all 12 Islamic months name

List of Islamic Months

  1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
  2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
  3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
  4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی
  5. Jumada al Awwal جمادی الاول
  6. Jumada al Thani جمادی الثانی
  7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
  8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
  9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
  10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
  11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
  12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

**** 

Each month begins approximately at the time of the new moon.

ND14-7-2023
189

How many times the word Zakat appears with Salat in the Holy Qur'an?

Correct Answer: 32
Show explanation
Explanation

32 times the word Zakat appears with Salat in the Holy Qur'an

About Zakat
Zakat became compulsory during the 2nd hijrah but its complete system was imposed in the year of the 8th hijrah.

The word Zakat occurs in the Holy Quran 32 Times

The word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs 22 times

Parents, Husband, wives, and children are not entitled to get Zakat.

A person who is liable to pay Zakat is called Sahib-e-Nisab

5 camels render are to par Zakat

40 goats render are to pay zakat

40 sheep render are to pay zakat

30-40 cows render are to pay Zakat

******

ND30-12-2022



190

Which Islamic country has became the first Arab country to launch mission on Mars?

Correct Answer: United Arab Emirates
Show explanation
Explanation

The Emirates Mars Mission is a United Arab Emirates Space Agency uncrewed space exploration mission to Mars.

The Hope probe was launched on 19 July 2020, and went into orbit around Mars on 9 February 2021

. The mission design, development, and operations are led by the Mohammed bin Rashid Space Centre.

191

Ihram consists of ______ clothes?

Correct Answer: 2 Cloths
Show explanation
Explanation

The Ihram attire consists of two white, simple pieces of cloth known as the Izar.



192

How many times Name Ahmed repeated in Quran?

Correct Answer: 1
Show explanation
Explanation

Name “Muhammad”, four times mentioned in the whole Quran.

1.       Surah ali-Imran: ayat 144.

2.       Surah al-Ahzab: ayat 40.

3.       Surah Muhammad: ayat 2.

4.       Surah al-Fath: ayat 29.

 

Name “Ahmed”, one time mentioned in the whole Quran.

1.       As-Saff: Ayat 6.

193

Treaty of hudybiyyah for how many years?

Correct Answer: 10
Show explanation
Explanation

The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah (صلح الحديبية) was an event that took place during the time of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca in January 628.

194

What was the total number of migrated people in the second migration to Abyssinia?

Correct Answer: 101
Show explanation
Explanation

First Migration comprised eleven men and four women (15 migrated people)

the Second Migration comprised eighty-three men and fourteen women. (101 migrated people)

Ja’far bin Abi Talib (RA) was the head of this group of immigrants (second migration)

Muslims migrated twice to Habshah (Abyssinia) first in 613 CE

and second in 616 CE to flee from the persecutions of Quraysh

195

Tauheed is an _____ language word.

توحید اسلام میں توحید کا ناقابل تقسیم وحدانیت کا تصور ہے۔

196

The first Charter of Human Rights is?

Correct Answer: Khutba-Hajjat-ul-Vida
Show explanation
Explanation

The first Charter of human rights is Khutba-hajj at-ul-Vida. 

..

Khutbah Hajjatul-Wida` is in fact the First Human Charter which was given by the Messenger of Allah (صلی اللہ علیہ و آلہ وسلم) to the whole of mankind on February, 632 AD.

The Hajj (pilgrimage) is known as 'Hajjatul Wida' or the Farewell Hajj.

The Prophet of Allah (PBUH) delivered a historical speech (Khutbah) on the ninth of Dhul Hijjah (the 12th and last month of the lunar year) on Friday; 10 years after Hijrah in the Uranah Valley of mount Arafat during the farewell pilgrimage.
197

How many kinds of Wahi are there?

Correct Answer: 2
Show explanation
Explanation

The literal meaning of "Wahi" is:  Allah's Message

The literal meaning of Wahi is inspiration, secret notification, or speedy message.

2 kinds of Wahi are there

1 Wahee-e-Matlu:

It means the revelation which God has transmitted in His own words and speech

2 Wahee-e-Ghair Matlu:

It is the revelation that Allah projected in the heart of the Holy Prophet.

ND11-2-2023



198

Who is called Umm-Ul-Masakeen?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Zainab (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

Umm-ul-Masakeen was the title given to one of the wives of the Prophet (SAW): Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA)

Umm-ul-Masakeen means mother of the poor.

Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA) was the fifth wife of Muhammad

Zaynab was the first of Muhammad’s wives who were not from the Quraysh tribe.
 
Zaynab's marriage to Muhammad lasted only a few months.

She died at the age of about thirty.

Muhammad carried her to Jannat al-Baqi


199

To stay in which ground is the major part of Hajj?

Correct Answer: Arafaat
Show explanation
Explanation

It is an important place in Islam because, during the Hajj, pilgrims spend the afternoon there on the ninth day of Dhu al-Hijjah.

Failure to be present in the plain of Arafat on the required day invalidates the pilgrimage.

200

Which two prayers are offered together at Muzdalifa on the 9th Zil-ul-Hajj?

Correct Answer: Maghrib-Isha
Show explanation
Explanation


Maghrib-Isha prayers are offered together at Muzdalifa on 9th  Zill Hajj

...

Muzdalifah was the place where Prophet Adam (AS) the Maghrib and Isha prayers together.

Muzdalifah is also called Al-Ibtah, meaning flat area.

...

between 49 and 70 pebbles are collected from the ground at Muzdalifah and thrown at the three pillars at Mina, which represent the devil.

Muzdalifah (Arabic: مزدلفة) which is also known as al-Mashʿar or al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām (المشعر الحرام) is a region between 'Arafat and Mina

    How many surah are named with animal names?

    Correct Answer: 6
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are more than 200 verses in the Quran dealing with animals

    The biggest animal mentioned in Holy Quran Elephant.
    The smallest animal mentioned in Holy Quran     Mosquito.

    The six chapters of the Quran are named after animals or insects:

    1.      Surat 2, Al Baqarah (The Cow);

    2.      Surat 6, Al Anaam (The Cattle),

    3.      Surat 16, Al Nahl (The Bees);

    4.      Surat 27, Al Naml (The Ants);

    5.      Surat 29, Al Ankabut (The Spider);

    6.      Surat 105, Al Fil.

      It was established by the Nadwatul Ulama, a council of Muslim scholars, on 26 September 1898.

      Hafeezullah was appointed the first principal of the Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama

      Explanation

      the thirtieth chapter, the number of its surahs is 37, most of them are short chapters

      قرآن پاک میں کل 114 سورتیں ہیں،

      آیات کی تعداد 6666 ہے

      جبکہ الفاظ کی تعداد 77701 ہے۔

      قرآن پاک کی سب سے بڑی سورۃ البقرہ ہے

      اور سب سے چھوٹی سورۃ الکوثر ہے 

      قرآن پاک میں موجوز سورتوں کو تنزیل کے اعتبار سے مکی اور مدنی میں تقسیم کیا گیا ہے

      ***

      ND28-1-2023


      216

      When did the lady named Zainab poisoned Holy Prophetﷺ?

      Correct Answer: Conquest of Khyber
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Zainab poisoned to  Holy Prophetﷺ at the time of Conquest of Khyber

      RY30012023

      217

      After a ------------- month at Madina, the change of Qibla occurred.

      Correct Answer: 18
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The change of Qibla occurred after the 18 months of migration of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) to Madina

       the change of Qibla occurred In 624 AD 

      During the Zohar prayer, the change of Qibla happened.

      In 2 Hijri years, Qibla has changed

      Masjid al-Qiblatain is one of the three oldest mosques in Islamic history

      The first Qibla Muslims prayed for is  Al-Aqsa mosque المسجد الأقصى (Bait Al-Maqdis بيت المقدس)

      ***

      ND29-1-2023

      218

      There are _____________ obligations for ablution.

      Correct Answer: 4
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      غسل کے تین فرائض ہیں۔

      1. کلی کرنا
      2. ناک میں پانی ڈالنا
      3. سارے جسم پر پانی بہانا

        وضو کے چار فرائض ہیں۔

        نمبر 1: چہرہ دھونا

        نمبر 2: دونوں ہاتھوں کو کہینیوں تک دھونا

        نمبر 3: چوتھائی سر کا مسح کرنا

        نمبر 4:پاوں کا ٹخنیوں سمیت دھونا

        Total number of Ummayad ruller

        Correct Answer: 14

        Ameer Muawiyah was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate, serving from 661 until his death. (30 years)

        Ameer Muawiyah Died:         680 AD, Damascus

        RY29012023

        221

        Hazrat Umer (R.A) embraced islam in ___________ Nabvi.

        Correct Answer: 6th
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb was the second Rashidun caliph,

        Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb ruled from August 634 until his assassination in 644.

        Umar was a senior companion and father-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad. 

        he became the first Muslim to openly pray at the Kaaba. 

         Umar participated in almost all battles

        At the time he embraced Islam, Hazrat Umar R.A. was about 32 years old.

        He was born in 584 AD.

        Hazrat Umar R.A. embraced Islam in 616 AD.

        ND29-1-2023

        222

        Laws about Orphanage revealed in which hijri?

        Correct Answer: 3
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Laws about orphanages were revealed to the Holy Prophet (SAW) in 3 AH.
        • Surah Baqarah, verse 83, mentions the rights of orphans.
        • The Arabic word for an orphan is "Yateem," meaning any child without a father.

        225

        قرآن مجید کا اختتام کس سورت پر ہوتا ہے

        Correct Answer: سورہ الناس
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        سورہ ناس قرآن کی 114ویں اور

        مکی سورتوں میں سے ہے

        جو تیسویں پارے میں واقع ہے۔

        سورہ ناس چارقل میں سے ایک ہے۔


        اعداد و شمار

        آیات: 6

        الفاظ: 20

        حروف: 78

        ND28-1-2023

        226

        قرآن پاک میں کس عورت کا نام آیا ہے

        Correct Answer: 25 years
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        ضرت عیسی علیہ السلام کو اللہ تعالی نے بغیر والد کے حضرت مریم  رضی اللہ عنہا کے بطن مبارک سے اپنے حکم سے پیدافرمایاتھا،

        حضرت مریم علیہا السلام ایک انتہائی شریف، عفیفہ اور پارسا خاتون تھیں

         انبیاء کے خاندان سے تھیں۔

        قرآن میں ایک پوری سورۃ (سورۃ مریم)ان کے نام سے موجود ہے

        ***

        ND28-1-2023


        227

        Hazrat Khadija (رضہ) remained married monogamously with Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ for ____ years.

        Correct Answer: 25 years
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hazrat Khadija was 40 years old when Hazrat Muhammad PBUH (25 years) married her.

        ****

        Khadijah married three times and had children from all her marriages.

        1.      she first married Atiq ibn 'A'idh ibn' Abdullah Al-Makhzumi

        2.      Second Malik ibn Nabash ibn Zargari ibn at-Tamimi.

        3.      Third Muhammad ﷺ

        ***

        ND28-1-2023

        **

        228

        What was the age of Hazrat Khadija (RA) when married to Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ?

        Correct Answer: 40
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Khadijah married three times and had children from all her marriages.

        1.      she first married Atiq ibn 'A'idh ibn' Abdullah Al-Makhzumi

        2.      Second Malik ibn Nabash ibn Zargari ibn at-Tamimi.

        3.      Third Muhammad ﷺ

        ****

        Hazrat Khadija was 40 years old when Hazrat Muhammad PBUH (25 years) married her.

        Khadijah died in "Ramadan of the year 10 after the Prophethood", i.e., in November AD 619.

        Khadijah bint Khuwaylid(R.A), was the first wife of the Islamic prophet Muhammadﷺ

        Khadijah bint Khuwaylid(R.A) was the first follower of the Islamic prophet Muhammadﷺ

        ****

        After the death of Hazrat Khadija, Prophet Muhammad (SAW) married a total of 12 women

        ***

        Hazrat Khadija (رضہ) bore all the children of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ except Ibrahim (رضہ)

        ***

        233

        Which is the 11th month of Islamic Calendar?

        Correct Answer: Dhu al-Qa'dah
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Must remember all 12 Islamic months name

        List of Islamic Months

        1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
        2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
        3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
        4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی
        5. Jumada al Awwal جمادی الاول
        6. Jumada al Thani جمادی الثانی
        7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
        8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
        9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
        10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
        11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
        12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

        **** 

        Each month begins approximately at the time of the new moon.

        **

        RK220123

        234

        Which is the 10th month of Islamic Calendar?

        Correct Answer: Shawwal
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Must remember all 12 Islamic months name

        List of Islamic Months

        1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
        2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
        3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
        4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی

        8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
        9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
        10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
        11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
        12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

        **** 

        Each month begins approximately at the time of the new moon.

        **

        239

        How many times name of Hazrat Ibrahim (AH) mentioned in the Holy Quran?

        Correct Answer: 69
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 69 times.
        important question : 
        Namrud ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into the fire
        Nimrod, the king of Babylon, commanded his men to throw Prophet Ibrahim (AS) into a huge pit of burning fire
        Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days
        Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)
        Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq
        Abraham had two children,
        1.      Isaac
        2.      Ismael,

        240

        میری قوم میں کسی غلطی پر کبھی اتفاق رائےنہیں ہو گا یہ کس کے الفاظ ہیں

        Correct Answer: حضرت محمد
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        ابو القاسم محمد بن عبد اللہ بن عبد المطلب (12 ربیع الاول عام الفیل / 8 جون 570ء یا 571ء – 12 ربیع الاول 10ھ / 8 جون 632ء) مسلمانوں کے آخری نبی ہیں۔ اہل اسلام کے نزدیک محمد تمام مذاہب کے پیشواؤں سے کامیاب ترین پیشوا تھے

        ٌ**********

        RY 13-01-2023

        241

        جہری نماز سے کیا مراد ہے؟

        Correct Answer: وہ نماز جس میں امام بلند آواز میں قرآت کرتا ہے
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        ایسی نماز جس میں امام بلند آواز میں قرآت کرے اسے جہری نماز کہا جاتا ہے جس میں فجر، مغرب اور عشاء شامل ہیں۔

        ایسی نماز جس میں امام بلند آواز میں قرآت نہ کرے اسے خفی نماز کہا جاتا ہے جس میں ظہر اور عصر شامل ہیں۔

        ٌ**********

        RY 13-01-2023

        242

        قرآن مجید کی سب سے چھوٹی سورۃ کونسی ہے؟

        Correct Answer: سورہ کوثر
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        قرآن مجید کی سب سے چھوٹی سورت سورہ کوثر

        جبکہ سب سے بڑی سورت سورہ البقرہ ہے۔

        RY 13-01-2023

        243

        نزول قرآن کا مدنی دور کتنا تھا؟

        Correct Answer: دس سال
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        جو قرآنی آیات مدینہ میں نازل ہوئیں انہیں مدنی سورہ کہا جاتا ہے اور جو سورہ مکہ میں نازل ہوئیں انہیں مکی سورہ کہا جاتا ہے۔

        ٌ**********

        RY 13-01-2023

        244

        قرآن مجید میں نماز کے ساتھ ساتھ ـــــــــــــــــــــــ کا بھی ذکر آیا ہے۔

        Show explanation
        Explanation

        245

        Alsaba Almasani is the name of Surah _____

        Correct Answer: Fatiha
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        One of the names of al-Fatihah is Sab'ul Mathani سبع المثاني which means the Repeated Seven.

        It is named so because it is most often repeated seven ayats .
